architecture.summary: A hierarchical two-level system combining a multi-modal LLM (vision + text) for high-level surgical task planning and reasoning with a lower-level deep reinforcement learning model for motion planning and execution. The LLM receives visual input from the surgical scene, reasons about dynamic conditions (blood clots, active bleeding), and outputs task decisions that guide the RL agent's real-time control of a surgical robot for blood suction.

biblio.title: From Decision to Action in Surgical Autonomy: Multi-Modal Large Language Models for Robot-Assisted Blood Suction

claims.statement: Using a multi-modal LLM as a higher-level reasoning unit can account for surgical complexities to achieve a level of reasoning previously unattainable in robot-assisted surgeries
The multi-modal LLM integration enables reasoning and adaptation to changes in the environment, including blood clots and active bleeding, to influence decision-making in autonomous blood suction
Multi-modal LLMs enhance contextual understanding and decision-making in robotic-assisted surgeries
